title: Intensely Savory Norwegian Potato Casserole
description: A savory and comforting lacto-vegetarian Norwegian dish featuring tamarind paste, this casserole is perfect for a cold winter night. It is dense and full of flavor, sure to become a family favorite.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot of salted water, boil the sliced potatoes for 5 minutes, then immediately submerge them in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
In a large skillet, saute the chopped onion in butter until softened.
In a large bowl, whisk together the tamarind paste, vegetable broth, milk, flour, salt, and black pepper.
Add the cooked potatoes and sauteed onion to the bowl and stir until they are well coated with the tamarind mixture.
Transfer the potato mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and cover with aluminum foil.
Bake for 30 minutes, then remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
